heroHeadline: Day 643: US Ambassador Previews Federal Indictments of Cartel-Linked Politicians in Los Mochis; CJNG Shock-Force Commander Captured
heroSubtitle: 643 days of cartel civil war — April 28: US Ambassador Ronald Johnson visited Los Mochis, Sinaloa, to announce the Trump administration's anti-corruption campaign will advance beyond visa bans to potential federal indictments of Mexican politicians with cartel ties, directly targeting Sinaloa's political landscape where Governor Rocha Moya's US visa was revoked over the Cuén murder. April 29: Audias Flores Silva ('El Jardinero'), CJNG's head of security and commander of Grupo Élite/Delta shock forces deployed to support Chapitos in Sinaloa, was captured in Nayarit — the third senior CJNG command layer removed since El Mencho's February 2026 death, effectively dismantling the CJNG-Chapitos military alliance. Seven Sinaloa meth traffickers also sentenced in US federal court (Kansas City). El Mayo EDNY sentencing set for May 18. Running conflict toll: ~2,425+ since Sep 9, 2024.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae, La Jornada, CBC News, Excelsior, El Diario del Noroeste, White House ONDCP, DOJ, El Informador, El Imparcial, Latin Times, Zeta Tijuana, NBC Los Angeles, KTLA, Washington Times, NBC Chicago, UPI, US State Department, CNN, Washington Post, CBS News, Expansión Política, La Razón, El Financiero, Latinus, Ejecentral, El Diario de Chihuahua, NTD, Eurasia Review, Ground News, El Sol de Sinaloa, Texas Public Radio / KVIA
